meta.title: UK Gap Map graph export
meta.note: Gap statuses are frozen at generation time; the live record is data/attempts.json in the repo. Node types: outcome, domain, gap. Edge types: in-domain, also-in, see-also, blocks (gap blocks outcome). Method kin: Discourse Graphs (discoursegraphs.com) and DSV's Outcomes Graph.

nodes.label: Communities can own and run local assets and services
nodes.line: The pub, the shop, the hall and the services around them can pass into community hands and stay there.

nodes.label: Every young person has a visible route in
nodes.line: Nobody drops off the radar at 16, and work, skills and participation are findable from any postcode.

nodes.label: Public money is traceable and contestable
nodes.line: Spending, contracts and influence can be followed by anyone, and misuse has consequences.

nodes.label: People control their own data and identity
nodes.line: Records, identifiers and communications answer to the people they describe.

nodes.label: Britain can run its critical digital infrastructure at home
nodes.line: The software, compute and models the country depends on do not sit solely in someone else's jurisdiction.

nodes.label: The debt is understood, and the unborn are not billed silently
nodes.line: What is owed, what servicing costs, and who pays are plain public knowledge shaping every fiscal choice.

nodes.label: Neighbourhoods stay functional when systems fail
nodes.line: When the mast, the grid or the supply chain breaks, the street has a practised fallback.

nodes.label: Health, care and prevention work close to home
nodes.line: The years of healthy life you get stop depending on the borough you were born in.

nodes.label: Justice is reachable when rights are breached
nodes.line: A right you cannot enforce is not narrowed quietly into a privilege.

nodes.label: Local economies grow and local government can act
nodes.line: Councils and regions hold the tools, data and revenue to fix what is in front of them.

nodes.label: The AI transition is prepared for, not suffered
nodes.line: Displacement, assurance and capability questions get answered before they harden into crises.

Place-based philanthropy outside London lacks endowed capital
Community organising has no long-term core-funding vehicle

No open national address file: PAF remains a proprietary Royal Mail asset

No at-scale 'first vote' programme for 1.6 million newly enfranchised 16-17-year-olds

After zonal pricing's rejection, no working locational signal for siting demand or generation
Secure research-data infrastructure for diagnosing the growth problem is going backwards
No standing mass-transit delivery unit or rolling tram programme for second cities

The Statutory Debt Repayment Plan: legislated in 2018, still switched off
Debt advice is funded by a levy on lenders who no longer cause the debt

No lawful retention and deletion regime for the 19 million custody images feeding facial recognition

No independent regulator, redress scheme or offline guarantee for the digital identity system
